grades.expectation: Response mentions checking competentperson.co.uk to verify if installer was registered
grades.reason: Response mentions checking FENSA and CERTASS websites directly but does not mention the competentperson.co.uk central registry

grades.expectation: Response mentions FENSA specifically as the relevant competent person scheme for windows
grades.reason: FENSA is prominently mentioned throughout as a competent person scheme for windows, with direct reference to checking their website

grades.expectation: Response distinguishes between self-certification (competent person) and local authority routes
grades.reason: Clearly explains that FENSA/CERTASS allow self-certification while non-registered installers must notify LABC for inspection

grades.expectation: Response warns about the 'no approach' rule before pursuing indemnity insurance
grades.reason: Explicitly states the 'Golden Rule' warning client not to contact Local Authority as it would void indemnity insurance

grades.expectation: Response mentions Part L (energy efficiency/insulation) as relevant regulation for windows
grades.reason: Specifically mentions Part L (Thermal Efficiency) as one of the key Building Regulations requirements for replacement windows
